title: J&K Extends Summer Vacations Till July 26 for Schools in Kashmir, Winter Zones of Jammu
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo) Jammu and Kashmir
full_text:
The Jammu and Kashmir government has once again extended the summer vacations for all schools in the Kashmir Division and Winter Zones of Jammu Division till the end of this week. As per the information shared by Sakina Itoo, Minister for School/Hr Education, Health & Medical Education and Social Welfare, Government of J&K.
She stated that all schools will remain closed till July 26. This will be applicable for all Government and recognised private schools in the Kashmir Division and Winter Zones of Jammu Division.
"Keeping the safety and well-being of students, teachers, and their families as our foremost priority, the summer vacation for all Government and recognized private schools in the Kashmir Division and Winter Zones of Jammu Division is hereby extended up to 26th July, 2026," Itoo posted on her X handle.
She further clarified that "schools will now reopen on Monday, 27th July, 2026."
"I request everyone to stay safe, remain vigilant, and follow the advisories issued by the concerned authorities. Wishing everyone good health and safety," the Minister added.
Sakina Itoo's tweet
The government had earlier announced the summer vacations on July 6, 2026, in view of intense heatwave conditions. All schools were scheduled to reopen on July 18, but the extreme weather conditions forced the authorities to announce further closures. Due to this, Sakin Itoo had on July 20 (Monday) announced that all Government and recognised private schools in the Kashmir Division and the winter zones of Jammu Division will remain closed till July 22, 2026 (Wednesday).
Now, due to the persistent heatwaves, the government has decided to further extend the summer vacations for all Government and recognised private schools in the Kashmir Division and Winter Zones of Jammu Division. till Sunday (July 26).
Meanwhile, all government and private educational institutions in Himachal Pradesh's Kangra, Mandi and Sirmaur districts were also ordered to remain closed on July 20 and 21 in view of a red alert issued by the weather department, according to the PTI reports.
For latest updates on school closures across India, keep visiting https://www.timesnownews.com/education.
